rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Use Pseudonym; Memo ** Redacted **
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 5, 2019, LINE 1 RESPONDENT STAN SCHULDINER'S MOTION To Use Pseudonym DENIED. Stan Schuldiner has used his real name in pleadings throughout this 45-month litigation, which is now on appeal. His motion to now use a pseudonym is DENIED. I agree with the order denying Schuldiner's prior motion for the same relief. As Judge Quinn found, use of a pseudonym impairs important public rights to access court records (see, e.g., CRC 2.550(c)) and violates the requirement that a complaint include "names of all the parties" (CCP §422.40). Moreover, Schuldiner's new motion again fails to demonstrate the kind of prejudice that could outweigh public rights and statutory requirements. (See Doe v. Lincoln Unified School Dist. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 758, 767.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for ITC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBU

rulings.Case Title: BUENOFRIO INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. NIESAR & VESTAL LLP A LIMITED PARTNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gerald Niesar, Oscar Escobar, Niesar & Vestal, Llp Notice Of Motion Contesting The Claim Of Privilege Assserted By Non-Party The Central Valley Fund, L.P. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure, Section 2031.285(D)(1).
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 5, 2019, LINE 13 Defendants Gerald Niesar, Oscar Escobar, and Niesar & Vestal, Llp's Motion Contesting The Claim Of Privilege Assserted By Non-Party The Central Valley Fund, L.P. Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure, Section 2031.285(D)(1) This discovery dispute regards an attorney-client-privileged e-mail into which a non-privileged text message was apparently pasted electronically. Non-party CVF Capital Partners, Inc. (which produced, then clawed back, the entire e-mail) and defendants stipulate that (1) CVP is not asserting the privilege as to the text message and (2) the privilege is not waived as to the rest of the e-mail. I adopt the judge pro tem's recommendation that the stipulation should be accepted. In opposition, plaintiffs rely on Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 725. But that case dealt with "piece-mealing" a 22-page opinion letter for privilege purposes, and not two distinct communications like those here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBU

rulings.Case Title: NP APARTMENTS, LLC. VS. ASSOCIATED MATERIALS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Exclude Opinions Of Plaintiffs Expert Witnesses Or In The Alternative To Compel Depositions Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 5, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED MATERIALS, LLC's Motion To Exclude Opinions Of Plaintiffs Expert Witnesses Or In The Alternative To Compel Depositions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The tentative ruling exceeds the system character limits. The Pro Tem Judge will email the parties and/or their counsel the tentative ruling prior to 3 pm. If any party does not receive the tentative ruling by 3 pm, please email the Pro Tem Judge at pvestal@nvlawllp.com and cc: all other parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion{s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion, and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VINNEL THOMAS VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SAN FRANCISCO MEDICAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 5, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall: 1) serve verified responses, without objections, to defendant's first set of form interrogatories, special interrogatories and inspection demands within 20 days of the date of the court's order; and 2) pay $250 in discovery sanctions to the client trust account of defendant's counsel of record by May 15, 2019 if the responses are not timely served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. VS. C.H.A CORP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 5, 2019, LINE 5 CROSS-DEFENDANTS BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. and AMNON OSHRI'S DEMURRERS TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT OVERRULED. A complaint "need only allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action; each evidentiary fact that might eventually form part of the plaintiff's proof need not be alleged." (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges facts to state causes of action for contract breach and conversion; every "foundational fact" need not be pled. (Id.) Oral representations and a prior ongoing business relationship are explicitly alleged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the cross-complainants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBU

rulings.Case Title: SELASSIE FLORES VS. WILLIE L BROWN JR. MIDDLE SCHOOL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 5, 2019, LINE 9, Demurrer 1 of 2 DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to the first cause of action for negligent supervision is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND; plaintiff submitted no argument opposing this demurrer. The school district's demurrer to the third cause of action for vicarious liability is OVERRULED. The district could be vicariously liable if the teacher's assault arose from a work-related conflict or event, such as a dispute with the student during class time, and such facts are adequately pled. (See, e.g., Lisa M. v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (1995) 12 Cal.4th 291, 300; Carr v. Wm. C. Crowell Co. (1946) 28 Cal.2d 652; Rodgers v. Kemper Constr. Co. (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 608, 621; Stansell v. Safeway Stores (1941) 44 Cal.App.2d 822; Cmplt. 19-20.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the District is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = RBU

rulings.Case Title: SELASSIE FLORES VS. WILLIE L BROWN JR. MIDDLE SCHOOL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 5, 2019, LINE 9, Demurrer 2 of 2 DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The City and the San Francisco Unified School District are separate entities, and the complaint states no facts indicating potential liability by the City as opposed to the school district or school. (See, e.g., Cal. Const. art. IX, §14; SF Charter art. VII, §8.100; Evid. Code, § 451(a); Jackson v. Board of Ed. of City of Los Angeles (1967) 250 Cal.App.2d 856, 858-59.)
